title: Fix project_name in devcontainer.json template
description:
## Goal
Use {{ project_name }} in devcontainer.json.liquid instead of hardcoded "Rust-Bucket".

## Task
Update templates/.devcontainer/devcontainer.json.liquid line 3:
- Change: "name": "Rust-Bucket"
- To: "name": "{{ project_name }}"

Also update the generated .devcontainer/devcontainer.json to match.

## Success Criteria
- Template uses {{ project_name }}
- Generated file shows "Rust-Bucket" (for this repo)
- All tests pass

title: Templatize Dockerfile version dependencies
description:
## Goal
Replace hardcoded version numbers in Dockerfile template with variables.

## Variables to Add
1. rust_version (default: "1.92") - Rust Docker image version
2. debian_release (default: "bookworm") - Debian release name
3. go_version (default: "1.24.2") - Go version for Beads installation
4. beads_version (default: "v0.46.0") - Beads version tag

## Files to Update
- templates/.devcontainer/Dockerfile.liquid
- .devcontainer/Dockerfile
- src/config.rs (add fields to Config struct)
- src/generator.rs (pass new variables to template engine)

## Success Criteria
- Template uses variables instead of hardcoded versions
- Config struct extended with new fields
- All tests pass

title: Verify and fix Go installation in Dockerfile for beads v0.46.0
description:
## Goal
Verify that the Dockerfile correctly installs Go 1.24+ for beads v0.46.0, not the older apt golang-go package.

## Problem
beads v0.46.0 requires Go 1.24.0 (with toolchain go1.24.11), but if the Dockerfile still uses `apt-get install golang-go`, it would install Go 1.21 or 1.22, causing build failures.

## Tasks
1. Read the current Dockerfile and Dockerfile.liquid template
2. Verify they do NOT use `apt-get install golang-go`
3. Verify they download Go 1.24+ directly from golang.org
4. If any issues found, fix them
5. Ensure template and generated file are in sync

## Success Criteria
- Dockerfile does NOT use apt's golang-go
- Dockerfile downloads Go 1.24+ from go.dev
- Template and generated file match
- All tests pass

title: Add role assumption and delegation clarifications to AGENTS.md
description:
Add clarifications to AGENTS.md template (templates/AGENTS.md.liquid) about:

1. Default role assumption: if an agent doesn't know its role, assume Coordinator and read WORKFLOW.md
2. Delegation enforcement: Coordinators must delegate all coding work via beads, never code directly

Reference the existing clarifications in WORKFLOW.md for consistency. The new text should be added to the 'Agent roles' section.

Files to read:
- templates/AGENTS.md.liquid (the template to modify)
- templates/WORKFLOW.md.liquid (for reference on the clarifications already added)

After editing, run 'cargo run -- apply' to regenerate AGENTS.md from the template.

title: Fix panic! violations in apply.rs tests
description:
Replace panic!() calls in test assertions with assert!(matches!(...)) pattern.

**Locations (3 violations):**
- src/apply.rs:256 - test_apply_init_not_rust_crate
- src/apply.rs:276 - test_apply_init_not_git_repo
- src/apply.rs:300 - test_apply_init_conflicts_without_force

**Pattern to fix:**
Replace:
```rust
match result.unwrap_err() {
    ExpectedError => {}
    e => panic!("Expected ExpectedError, got: {:?}", e),
}
```

With:
```rust
assert!(
    matches!(result.unwrap_err(), ExpectedError),
    "Expected ExpectedError, got: {:?}", result
);
```

Or use assert_matches! macro if available.

**Files to read:**
- src/apply.rs (test module starting around line 220)
- STYLE_GUIDE.md

**Success criteria:**
- All 3 panic! calls in apply.rs tests are replaced
- cargo test passes
- ratchet check shows no-panic budget reduced by 3

title: Fix panic! violations in generator.rs tests
description:
Replace panic!() calls in test assertions with assert!(matches!(...)) pattern.

**Locations (3 violations):**
- src/generator.rs:289 - test_conflict_detection
- src/generator.rs:333 - test_nonexistent_template_directory
- src/generator.rs:390 - test_template_syntax_error

**Pattern to fix:**
Replace:
```rust
match result.unwrap_err() {
    ExpectedError => {}
    _ => panic!("Expected ExpectedError"),
}
```

With:
```rust
assert!(
    matches!(result.unwrap_err(), ExpectedError),
    "Expected ExpectedError"
);
```

Or use assert_matches! macro if available.

**Files to read:**
- src/generator.rs (test module starting around line 170)
- STYLE_GUIDE.md

**Success criteria:**
- All 3 panic! calls in generator.rs tests are replaced
- cargo test passes
- ratchet check shows no-panic budget reduced by 3

Add bead commit requirement to Coordinator workflow
description:
## Goal
Add instruction to WORKFLOW.md that Coordinators must commit changes to .beads/issues.jsonl when they create or update beads.

## Tasks
1. Update WORKFLOW.md "Operational notes" or "Coordinator workflow rules" section to include:
   - When creating or updating beads, commit the changes to .beads/issues.jsonl
   - This ensures bead state is tracked in version control

2. Hoist changes to templates/WORKFLOW.md.liquid

## Success Criteria
- cargo fmt --check passes
- cargo clippy passes
- cargo nextest run passes
- WORKFLOW.md documents the bead commit requirement
- Template matches

title: Fold idea 3: delegation-prompt hygiene (coordinator template)
description: Add 'Delegation prompt convention' to coordinator.md.liquid: don't re-state standing rules in every delegation prompt; subagents already read AGENTS.md + their role file; keep prompts to bead id, goal, files, pre-attempt commit hash, task-specific constraints, pointer to AGENTS.md. From ratchets reflection.

title: Fix Go version incompatibility in Dockerfile for beads installation
description:
## Goal
Fix the devcontainer build failure caused by Debian's golang-go package (Go 1.19) being too old for beads v0.46.0 which requires Go 1.24.

## Problem
Error: go.mod:3: invalid go version '1.24.0': must match format 1.23
The beads project requires Go 1.24+, but Debian bookworm ships Go 1.19.

## Tasks
1. Update Dockerfile template to install a newer Go version instead of apt's golang-go
2. Options to consider:
   - Download Go binary directly from golang.org
   - Use a multi-stage build with golang:1.24 image
   - Install via snap or other package manager
3. Update generated Dockerfile to match template

## Success Criteria
- Dockerfile builds successfully
- bd version works after container build
- All tests pass

5. Regenerate AGENTS.md with the new template

## Success Criteria
- Template uses {{ project_name }} variable
- Config includes project_name field
- All tests pass

title: Auto-detect project name from Cargo.toml during apply_init
description:
## Goal
Improve UX by automatically detecting the project name from the target project's Cargo.toml during apply_init, rather than hardcoding "Rust-Bucket".

## Background
bd-6 added the project_name template variable, but apply_init hardcodes "Rust-Bucket". This means users must manually edit rust-bucket.toml after first run.

## Tasks
1. In apply_init, parse the target directory's Cargo.toml to extract package name
2. Use extracted name as default for project_name
3. Optionally prompt user to confirm/change the project name

## Success Criteria
- Running rust-bucket apply on a new project auto-detects the project name
- Generated AGENTS.md shows correct project name
- All tests pass

title: Initialize Rust project structure
description:
## Goal
Create the foundational Rust project structure for rust-bucket.

## Tasks
1. Create `Cargo.toml` with:
   - name = "rust-bucket"
   - version = "0.1.0"
   - edition = "2024"
   - Required dependencies:
     - clap = { version = "4", features = ["derive"] }
     - serde = { version = "1", features = ["derive"] }
     - toml = "0.8"
     - rust-embed = "8"
     - thiserror = "2"
     - tempfile = "3"

2. Create `src/main.rs` with:
   - `#![forbid(unsafe_code)]` at the top
   - Minimal main() that just prints version for now
   - `mod lib;` or use the library crate

3. Create `src/lib.rs` with:
   - `#![forbid(unsafe_code)]` at the top
   - Module declarations (as stubs/comments for now):
     - `pub mod cli;`
     - `pub mod config;`
     - `pub mod generator;`
     - `pub mod templates;`
     - `pub mod verify;`

4. Create stub module files:
   - `src/cli.rs` (empty, just a comment)
   - `src/config.rs` (empty, just a comment)
   - `src/generator.rs` (empty, just a comment)
   - `src/templates.rs` (empty, just a comment)
   - `src/verify.rs` (empty, just a comment)

## Success Criteria
- `cargo check` passes
- `cargo fmt --check` passes
- `cargo clippy` passes (no warnings)
- All files have `#![forbid(unsafe_code)]` where applicable

## References
- Read ARCHITECTURE.md for component overview
- Read STYLE_GUIDE.md for coding conventions

title: Create templates directory structure
description:
## Goal
Create the templates/ directory structure that will be embedded into the rust-bucket binary.

## Tasks
1. Create `templates/` directory at repo root

2. Create subdirectories matching the output structure:
   - `templates/.beads/` (for beads config)
   - `templates/.config/` (for nextest.toml)
   - `templates/.devcontainer/` (for devcontainer files)

3. Create a `templates/cargo-generate.toml` manifest file:
   ```toml
   [template]
   cargo_generate_version = ">=0.18.0"
   
   [placeholders]
   test_timeout = { type = "string", prompt = "Test timeout in seconds", default = "120" }
   rust_bucket_version = { type = "string", prompt = "rust-bucket version" }
   ```

4. Create placeholder files (empty or minimal) in each directory to establish structure:
   - `templates/AGENTS.md.liquid`
   - `templates/STYLE_GUIDE.md.liquid`
   - `templates/WORKFLOW.md.liquid`
   - `templates/TESTING.md.liquid`
   - `templates/.config/nextest.toml.liquid`
   - `templates/deny.toml.liquid`
   - `templates/rustfmt.toml.liquid`
   - `templates/.devcontainer/Dockerfile.liquid`
   - `templates/.devcontainer/devcontainer.json.liquid`
   - `templates/.beads/config.yaml.liquid`

## Success Criteria
- Directory structure exists
- All placeholder files exist (can be empty)
- cargo-generate.toml is valid TOML

## References
- Read ARCHITECTURE.md section on Template Pack
- Existing managed file list in README.md

title: Create AGENTS.md template
description:
## Goal
Convert the existing AGENTS.md into a Liquid template.

## Tasks
1. Read the current `/code/AGENTS.md` file

2. Create `templates/AGENTS.md.liquid` based on it:
   - Add version stamp at top: `<!-- Generated by rust-bucket v{{ rust_bucket_version }}. DO NOT EDIT BY HAND. -->`
   - Keep all existing content
   - No other template variables needed for v1

3. The template should render to nearly identical content as the original

## Success Criteria
- `templates/AGENTS.md.liquid` exists
- Has version stamp comment at top
- Content matches original AGENTS.md (minus the hardcoded version)

## References
- Read existing AGENTS.md for content
- ARCHITECTURE.md for version stamping requirement

title: Create .config/nextest.toml template
description:

Create the nextest.toml template for cargo-nextest configuration.

## Tasks

   - Version stamp comment at top: `# Generated by rust-bucket v{{ rust_bucket_version }}. DO NOT EDIT BY HAND.`
   - Global timeout setting using template variable:
     ```toml
     [profile.default]
     global-timeout = "{{ test_timeout }}s"
     ```

2. This file configures cargo-nextest per TESTING.md requirements

## Success Criteria
- `templates/.config/nextest.toml.liquid` exists
- Has version stamp comment
- Uses `{{ test_timeout }}` for the global timeout
- Valid TOML when rendered

title: Create deny.toml template
description:
## Goal
Create the cargo-deny configuration template.

## Tasks
1. Create `templates/deny.toml.liquid` with:
   - Version stamp comment at top: `# Generated by rust-bucket v{{ rust_bucket_version }}. DO NOT EDIT BY HAND.`
   - Reasonable default cargo-deny configuration:
     - Advisories check enabled
     - License check with common permissive licenses allowed (MIT, Apache-2.0, BSD-*)
     - Bans section (empty but present)
     - Sources section (crates.io only)

2. This is a static template for v1 (no user-configurable variables)

## Success Criteria
- `templates/deny.toml.liquid` exists
- Has version stamp comment
- Valid TOML when rendered
- Sensible default deny configuration

## References
- cargo-deny documentation: https://embarkstudios.github.io/cargo-deny/

title: Fold idea 6: commit-message escaping (coordinator template)
description: Add 'Commit-message escaping' to coordinator.md.liquid: apostrophes in heredocs break git commit -m; write message to a temp file and use git commit -F. Generalize ratchets-specific example wording. From ratchets reflection.

title: Implement config module
description:
## Goal
Implement the config module for loading/saving rust-bucket.toml.

## Tasks
1. Implement `src/config.rs` with:

2. Define the `Config` struct:
   ```rust
   #[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]
   pub struct Config {
       /// Version of rust-bucket that generated this config
       pub rust_bucket_version: String,
       /// Test timeout in seconds (default: 120)
       pub test_timeout: u32,
   }
   ```

3. Implement `Config::default()` with:
   - rust_bucket_version from env!("CARGO_PKG_VERSION")
   - test_timeout = 120

4. Implement loading:
   ```rust
   pub fn load(path: &Path) -> Result<Config, ConfigError>
   ```

5. Implement saving:
   ```rust
   pub fn save(&self, path: &Path) -> Result<(), ConfigError>
   ```

6. Define `ConfigError` enum using thiserror:
   - IoError
   - ParseError
   - ValidationError

7. The saved file should include a comment header:
   ```toml
   # Generated by rust-bucket vX.Y.Z. DO NOT EDIT BY HAND.
   ```

## Success Criteria
- `cargo check` passes
- `cargo clippy` passes
- Unit tests for load/save round-trip
- No unwrap() or expect() in production code

## References
- Read ARCHITECTURE.md for config requirements
- Read STYLE_GUIDE.md for error handling rules

title: Implement CLI module
description:
## Goal
Implement the CLI module using clap for argument parsing.

## Tasks
1. Implement `src/cli.rs` with clap derive macros

2. Define the CLI structure:
   ```rust
   #[derive(Parser)]
   #[command(name = "rust-bucket")]
   #[command(about = "Rust-first project bootstrapper for AI-first engineering")]
   pub struct Cli {
       #[command(subcommand)]
       pub command: Commands,
   }
   
   #[derive(Subcommand)]
   pub enum Commands {
       /// Apply rust-bucket to the current directory
       Apply {
           /// Force overwrite of existing managed files
           #[arg(long)]
           force: bool,
       },
   }
   ```

3. Implement interactive prompting:
   ```rust
   pub fn prompt_test_timeout() -> Result<u32, CliError>
   ```
   - Read from stdin
   - Validate input is a positive integer
   - Default to 120 if empty input

4. Define `CliError` enum using thiserror

## Success Criteria
- `cargo check` passes
- `cargo clippy` passes
- `rust-bucket --help` shows usage
- `rust-bucket apply --help` shows apply options
- No unwrap() or expect() in production code

## References
- Read ARCHITECTURE.md for CLI requirements
- clap documentation: https://docs.rs/clap/latest/clap/

title: Implement template embedding module
description:
## Goal
Implement the template embedding module using rust-embed.

## Tasks
1. Implement `src/templates.rs` with rust-embed

2. Define the embedded templates:
   ```rust
   #[derive(RustEmbed)]
   #[folder = "templates/"]
   pub struct Templates;
   ```

3. Implement template extraction to temp directory:
   ```rust
   pub fn extract_to_temp() -> Result<PathBuf, TemplateError>
   ```
   - Create a temp directory using `tempfile`
   - Extract all embedded files to the temp directory
   - Return the temp directory path

4. Implement listing of managed files:
   ```rust
   pub fn managed_files() -> Vec<&'static str>
   ```
   - Return list of all files that rust-bucket manages
   - This is used for conflict detection

5. Define `TemplateError` enum using thiserror

## Success Criteria
- `cargo check` passes
- `cargo clippy` passes
- Unit test that extraction creates expected files
- No unwrap() or expect() in production code

## References
- Read ARCHITECTURE.md for template embedding requirements
- rust-embed documentation: https://docs.rs/rust-embed/latest/rust_embed/
- tempfile documentation: https://docs.rs/tempfile/latest/tempfile/

title: Implement generator module with cargo-generate
description:
## Goal
Implement the generator module using cargo-generate as a library.

## Tasks
1. Add cargo-generate as a dependency in Cargo.toml:
   ```toml
   cargo-generate = "0.22"
   ```

2. Implement `src/generator.rs`:

3. Create a function to render templates:
   ```rust
   pub fn render(
       template_dir: &Path,
       output_dir: &Path,
       config: &Config,
       overwrite: bool,
   ) -> Result<Vec<PathBuf>, GeneratorError>
   ```
   - Use cargo-generate programmatically
   - Pass config values as template variables:
     - rust_bucket_version
     - test_timeout
   - Return list of generated files

4. Handle overwrite modes:
   - If overwrite=false, fail if any target file exists
   - If overwrite=true, replace existing files

5. Define `GeneratorError` enum using thiserror:
   - TemplateError
   - IoError
   - ConflictError(Vec<PathBuf>) - list of conflicting files

6. If cargo-generate library API is difficult:
   - Document the issue
   - Consider falling back to direct liquid template rendering
   - Create a follow-up bead if needed

## Success Criteria
- `cargo check` passes
- `cargo clippy` passes
- Can render templates with variable substitution
- No unwrap() or expect() in production code

## References
- Read ARCHITECTURE.md for generator requirements
- cargo-generate source: https://github.com/cargo-generate/cargo-generate
- If blocked, create bead to analyze cargo-generate API

title: Implement managed files registry
description:
## Goal
Implement the managed files registry that tracks which files rust-bucket controls.

## Tasks
1. Add to `src/generator.rs` or create `src/managed.rs`:

2. Define the complete list of managed files:
   ```rust
   pub const MANAGED_FILES: &[&str] = &[
       "AGENTS.md",
       "STYLE_GUIDE.md",
       "WORKFLOW.md",
       "TESTING.md",
       ".config/nextest.toml",
       "deny.toml",
       "rustfmt.toml",
       ".devcontainer/Dockerfile",
       ".devcontainer/devcontainer.json",
       ".beads/config.yaml",
   ];
   ```

3. Implement conflict detection:
   ```rust
   pub fn check_conflicts(target_dir: &Path) -> Vec<PathBuf>
   ```
   - Check which managed files already exist
   - Return list of existing files (conflicts)

4. Implement file existence check:
   ```rust
   pub fn has_rust_bucket_toml(target_dir: &Path) -> bool
   ```
   - Used to detect init vs update mode

## Success Criteria
- `cargo check` passes
- `cargo clippy` passes
- Conflict detection works correctly
- Unit tests for conflict detection

## References
- README.md for list of managed files
- ARCHITECTURE.md for overwrite policies

title: Implement verification module
description:
## Goal
Implement the verification module that runs cargo checks after generation.

## Tasks
1. Implement `src/verify.rs`:

2. Define verification steps:
   ```rust
   pub enum VerifyStep {
       Format,
       Clippy,
       Test,
   }
   ```

3. Implement running verification:
   ```rust
   pub fn run_all(target_dir: &Path) -> Result<VerifyReport, VerifyError>
   ```
   - Run `cargo fmt --check`
   - Run `cargo clippy --all-targets --all-features`
   - Run `cargo nextest run`
   - Collect results for each step

4. Define `VerifyReport` struct:
   ```rust
   pub struct VerifyReport {
       pub format: StepResult,
       pub clippy: StepResult,
       pub test: StepResult,
   }
   
   pub enum StepResult {
       Pass,
       Fail(String), // error message
       Skip(String), // reason skipped
   }
   ```

5. Handle cases where commands aren't available:
   - If cargo-nextest not installed, report skip
   - If no tests exist, report pass

6. Define `VerifyError` enum using thiserror

## Success Criteria
- `cargo check` passes
- `cargo clippy` passes
- Can run verification commands and report results
- No unwrap() or expect() in production code

## References
- TESTING.md for verification requirements
- AGENTS.md for Definition of Done

title: Implement init flow
description:
## Goal
Implement the first-time `rust-bucket apply` flow.

## Tasks
1. Implement in `src/lib.rs` or `src/apply.rs`:

2. Create the init flow function:
   ```rust
   pub fn apply_init(
       target_dir: &Path,
       force: bool,
   ) -> Result<ApplyResult, ApplyError>
   ```

3. Implement the flow per ARCHITECTURE.md:
   a. Assert Cargo.toml exists (this is a Rust crate)
   b. Assert .git/ exists (git init was done)
   c. Check for conflicts:
      - If conflicts exist and !force, fail with list of conflicts
      - If conflicts exist and force, warn and continue
   d. Prompt for choices (test_timeout)
   e. Create Config with choices
   f. Write rust-bucket.toml
   g. Extract templates to temp dir
   h. Render templates to target dir (overwrite=force)
   i. Run verification
   j. Return result

4. Define `ApplyResult` struct:
   ```rust
   pub struct ApplyResult {
       pub files_generated: Vec<PathBuf>,
       pub verification: VerifyReport,
   }
   ```

5. Define `ApplyError` enum:
   - NotRustCrate
   - NotGitRepo
   - ConflictingFiles(Vec<PathBuf>)
   - ConfigError
   - GeneratorError
   - VerifyError

## Success Criteria
- `cargo check` passes
- `cargo clippy` passes
- Init flow works end-to-end
- Proper error messages for each failure mode
- No unwrap() or expect() in production code

## References
- ARCHITECTURE.md apply first time flow
- WORKFLOW.md for verification requirements

title: Implement update flow
description:
## Goal
Implement the subsequent `rust-bucket apply` flow (update mode).

## Tasks
1. Add to `src/lib.rs` or `src/apply.rs`:

2. Create the update flow function:
   ```rust
   pub fn apply_update(
       target_dir: &Path,
   ) -> Result<ApplyResult, ApplyError>
   ```

3. Implement the flow per ARCHITECTURE.md:
   a. Load rust-bucket.toml
   b. Validate config (check version compatibility if needed)
   c. Pre-populate choices from config
   d. Prompt for any NEW choices not in config (none in v1)
   e. Update config with any new values
   f. Write updated rust-bucket.toml
   g. Extract templates to temp dir
   h. Render templates to target dir (overwrite=true)
   i. Run verification
   j. Return result

4. Ensure version stamp is updated in all files

## Success Criteria
- `cargo check` passes
- `cargo clippy` passes
- Update flow works end-to-end
- Existing config values are preserved
- All managed files are regenerated
- No unwrap() or expect() in production code

## References
- ARCHITECTURE.md apply subsequent times flow

title: Wire modules together and complete main
Add integration tests
Research cargo-generate library API
description:
## Goal
If generator module (bd-c001) encounters difficulties with cargo-generate as a library, research and document the API.

## Trigger
Only execute this bead if bd-c001 fails or escalates due to cargo-generate issues.

## Tasks
1. Clone or read cargo-generate source code:
   - https://github.com/cargo-generate/cargo-generate

2. Analyze the library API:
   - Find the main entry points for programmatic use
   - Document required structs and functions
   - Identify how to:
     - Provide template directory
     - Pass template variables
     - Control overwrite behavior
     - Handle errors

3. Create `docs/CARGO_GENERATE_API.md` with:
   - Summary of findings
   - Code examples for our use case
   - Any limitations or workarounds
   - Alternative approaches if library use is impractical

4. If cargo-generate library use is impractical:
   - Document why
   - Propose using `liquid` crate directly
   - Update ARCHITECTURE.md with new approach

## Success Criteria
- Documentation created if bead is triggered
- Clear recommendation for how to proceed

## References
- cargo-generate source code
- liquid crate: https://docs.rs/liquid/latest/liquid/

title: Clarify Coordinator workflow instructions
description:
## Goal
Make the Coordinator Agent workflow crystal clear in WORKFLOW.md, especially the delegation/judge/revert cycle.

## Tasks
1. Update WORKFLOW.md to clarify:
   - Coordinator delegates object-level work to ONE Coding Subagent at a time
   - After each Coding Subagent completes, Coordinator MUST run a Judge Subagent
   - Judge verifies correctness AND style guide compliance
   - If Judge passes: mark bead done, move to next
   - If Judge fails: git reset --hard to pre-attempt commit, reset bead state, retry (max 4 attempts)
   - Coordinator does NOT do coding work itself

2. Update the graphviz flowchart to reflect this clearly:
   - Add explicit JUDGE step after DELEGATE with clear decision flow
   - Show the Judge decision point
   - Clarify the revert/retry flow

3. Hoist all changes to templates/WORKFLOW.md.liquid

## Success Criteria
- cargo fmt --check passes
- cargo clippy passes
- cargo nextest run passes
- WORKFLOW.md clearly documents the Coordinator workflow
- Template is updated to match

## References
- Read existing WORKFLOW.md
- Read AGENTS.md for role definitions

title: Add AGENTS.md to READ_DOCS in workflow flowchart

title: Migrate issues.jsonl schema and pin beads version
description:
## Goal
Migrate .beads/issues.jsonl to bd 0.46.0 schema and pin the beads version in Dockerfile.

## Tasks
1. Migrate issues.jsonl schema:
   - Convert priority from string to int (P0->0, P1->1, P2->2, P3->3)
   - Rename body -> description
   - Add required fields: issue_type, created_at, updated_at
   - Remove/update incompatible fields

2. Pin beads version in Dockerfile template to 0.46.0

3. Update generated Dockerfile to match

## Success Criteria
- bd list works without errors
- bd create works
- All tests pass

title: Fold idea 5: durable bead authoring (tidy template)
description: Add 'Writing beads that age well' to tidy.md.liquid: anchor by name/symbol not count/line; state invariant not value; include a 'Done when' verification line. Neutralize ratchets-specific path examples to generic placeholders. From ratchets reflection.

title: Delete extraneous code comments per tightened RUST_STYLE_GUIDE Comments rule
description: Remove step-by-step narration, enumerated procedure doc-blocks, history/rationale comments per judge review. Preserve genuine maintainer info and real Errors/Arguments contracts.

title: Fold idea 1: bead-state ownership is Coordinator-exclusive (coordinator+coding templates)
description: Add to templates/.claude/agents/coordinator.md.liquid and coding.md.liquid: coding subagents must not run br update/close or commit under .beads/; only the Coordinator mutates/commits bead state, after Judge passes. Generic, additive doc sections. From ratchets reflection.
